tiers.name: Canon
tiers.description: Essential masterworks that define the color's cinematic identity
tiers.purpose: The definitive films that, when someone thinks of this color in cinema, these are the touchstones. Each film is a cultural landmark.
characteristics: Explicit mastery of color as narrative/emotional tool

curation.ranking: Manual rank order (1-30) based on importance and influence

ui_presentation.notes: Always shown at top of color page; most prominent visual treatment

tiers.name: Core
tiers.description: Important works that strongly represent the color's character
tiers.purpose: The backbone of each color's collection. Reliable, high-quality examples that demonstrate the color's range and versatility.
characteristics: Strong color identity with clear intentionality
May not be universally known but respected in cinephile circles

curation.ranking: Editorial tier_rank if set, else numeric score

ui_presentation.default_sort: By tier_rank if exists, else numeric score
ui_presentation.notes: Shown prominently after Canon tier; clear visual distinction

tiers.name: Strong
tiers.description: Solid examples with clear color identity and quality
tiers.purpose: The depth of each color's catalog. Good films with distinctive color work that may not be canonical but are worth exploring.
characteristics: May be less well-known or recent releases

ui_presentation.notes: Shown in scrollable list after Core tier; less visual emphasis but accessible

examples: Recent festival films with strong color work
International cinema with clear color identity

tiers.name: Peripheral
tiers.description: Valid but less distinctive examples; exploratory tier
tiers.purpose: The long tail. Films that technically fit the color but aren't essential viewing. Good for completionists and niche interests.
characteristics: Color present but not necessarily defining
May be good films with less distinctive color work
May be lesser-known works from great directors

ui_presentation.notes: Hidden by default; shown via 'Show More' or filter; minimal visual treatment

examples: Films with color present but not dominant
Lower-budget films with good color work

tiers.name: Uncertain
tiers.description: Films below quality threshold; not displayed publicly
tiers.purpose: Internal tier for films that don't meet inclusion criteria. Retained for analysis, debugging, and potential re-evaluation.

ui_presentation.notes: Not shown in public site; visible only in admin/debug views

tier_transition_rules.description: Rules for how films move between tiers over time

conditions: Score increases to ≥ 70 (via updated metadata or awards)
Cultural recognition increases (new festival selection, award)
Frame validation confirms stronger color presence
peripheral_to_strong.action: Automatic re-tier on next pipeline run

conditions: OR editorial decision to highlight film
OR sustained community engagement (if social features active)
strong_to_core.action: Automatic re-tier OR manual editorial promotion

conditions: Canon tier must have space (max 30 per color)
Making room for more significant film
Color assignment found to be incorrect
any_to_uncertain.action: Flag for review; remove from public tiers

canon.notes: Each film has explicit rank reflecting cultural/historical importance

core.notes: Hybrid approach allows editorial curation of key films while letting algorithm handle the rest

visual_hierarchy.canon: Largest thumbnails, gold accent, featured placement
visual_hierarchy.core: Medium thumbnails, silver accent, prominent placement
visual_hierarchy.strong: Standard thumbnails, bronze accent, scrollable list
visual_hierarchy.peripheral: Small thumbnails, minimal styling, collapsed by default

badge_design.peripheral: No badge or very subtle gray indicator
